resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def isFascinating(self, n: int) -> bool:
        s = str(n)
        t = str(2 * n)
        u = str(3 * n)
        v = s + t + u
        return len(set(v)) == 9 and '0' not in v
```
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def isFascinating(self, n: int) -> bool:
        s = str(n)
        t = str(2 * n)
        u = str(3 * n)
        v = s + t + u
        return len(set(v)) == 9 and '0' not in v
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently checks if the concatenated string contains all digits from 1 to 9 exactly once and does not contain any zeros. It utilizes string operations and set length to achieve this in constant time.
